# FHIR Query Set: Migraine Patient Population — Quality Improvement Initiative

## Strategy Overview

| Pillar | Resource | Code Systems Used |
|---|---|---|
| Migraine diagnoses | `Condition` | ICD-10-CM (`:below` hierarchy), SNOMED CT |
| Triptan prescriptions | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm (ingredient-level) |
| Triptan fills (adherence) | `MedicationDispense` | RxNorm (ingredient-level) |
| Patient-reported use | `MedicationStatement` | RxNorm (ingredient-level) |

Each query includes `_include` to pull the linked `Patient` resource in the same bundle, enabling direct population assembly.

---

## GROUP A — Condition (Diagnosis) Queries

### A1 · ICD-10-CM G43.x — Full Migraine Hierarchy (`:below` captures all subcodes)

> Covers: G43 · G43.0xx (without aura) · G43.1xx (with aura) · G43.4xx (hemiplegic) · G43.7xx (chronic) · G43.8xx (other) · G43.9xx (unspecified) — all intractable/non-intractable and status migrainosus variants

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G43&_include=Condition:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### A2 · ICD-10-CM G44.x — Other Headache Syndromes / "Headache Disorder" NOS

> Covers: G44.0 (cluster), G44.1 (vascular headache NEC), G44.2 (tension-type), G44.3 (post-traumatic), G44.4 (drug-induced), G44.8 (other specified). Use when providers document "headache disorder" without a specific migraine code.
> ⚠️ Broader than migraine alone — downstream clinical review recommended.

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G44&_include=Condition:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### A3 · SNOMED CT — Migraine Family + Headache Disorder

> Catches charts coded in SNOMED (common in EHRs with dual-coding or problem list SNOMED entries)

| SNOMED Code | Concept |
|---|---|
| `37796009` | Migraine (disorder) — parent concept |
| `4473006` | Migraine with aura (classical migraine) |
| `56097005` | Migraine without aura (common migraine) |
| `95661003` | Hemiplegic migraine |
| `25064002` | Headache (finding) — broad catch-all for "headache disorder" |

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|37796009,http://snomed.info/sct|4473006,http://snomed.info/sct|56097005,http://snomed.info/sct|95661003,http://snomed.info/sct|25064002&_include=Condition:patient&_count=1000
```

---

## GROUP B — MedicationRequest (Triptan Prescriptions)

> RxNorm **ingredient-level** codes are used so all formulations (oral, nasal, injectable, ODT) are captured regardless of dose form or strength.

### B1 · Sumatriptan — RxNorm `41493`

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### B2 · Rizatriptan — RxNorm `93750`

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|93750&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### B3 · Eletriptan — RxNorm `275033`

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|275033&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### B4 · Zolmitriptan — RxNorm `96128`

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|96128&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### B5 · Naratriptan — RxNorm `59209`

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|59209&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### B-Combined · All Five Triptans — Single Query (alternative to B1–B5)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|93750,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|275033,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|96128,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|59209&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=1000
```

---

## GROUP C — Supplemental Queries (Recommended for Adherence Initiative)

### C1 · MedicationDispense — Triptan Fills
> **Critical for adherence analysis**: compares prescriptions written (B-queries) vs. prescriptions filled. Patients with MedicationRequests but no MedicationDispense records are non-adherent at the fill step.

```
MedicationDispense?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|93750,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|275033,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|96128,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|59209&_include=MedicationDispense:patient&_count=1000
```

---

### C2 · MedicationStatement — Patient-Reported / Imported Triptan Use
> Captures triptans documented via patient portal, care transitions, or external records that never generated a local MedicationRequest

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|93750,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|275033,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|96128,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|59209&_include=MedicationStatement:patient&_count=1000
```

---

## Population Assembly Notes

| Topic | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Union logic** | Collect `Patient.id` values across all query result bundles; deduplicate by `Patient.id` for the final cohort |
| **`:below` modifier** | Requires the server's ICD-10-CM terminology to be loaded with hierarchy support; test with a known G43.009 record to confirm before relying on it |
| **Ingredient vs. clinical drug codes** | If your EHR stores medications at the clinical drug level (e.g., `sumatriptan 100 mg oral tablet` = RxNorm `858088`), add `_revinclude` or use `code:above` to walk up to the ingredient; the ingredient-level query may miss those records on some systems |
| **MedicationRequest status** | Default queries here return all statuses. Add `&status=active,completed` to focus on active/fulfilled prescriptions for adherence denominator |
| **Diagnosis-only patients** | Patients appearing in Group A **but not** Group B/C are migraine-diagnosed but not on triptans — a key sub-population for the QI initiative (untreated or using only non-triptan abortives) |
| **Triptan-only patients** | Patients in Group B/C **but not** Group A may have had diagnoses coded at another facility — worth flagging for chart review |
| **Pagination** | Use `_count=1000` and follow `Bundle.link[rel=next]` for populations exceeding the page size |

## Population Assembly Notes

| Consideration | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Deduplication** | Union all results by `Patient.id` (or `subject.reference`) across all 11 queries |
| **Active diagnoses only** | Append `&clinical-status=active` to Condition queries (A1–A6) if you want only current problem-list entries |
| **Historical prescriptions** | Remove `&status=active` from B1–B6 to include completed/stopped triptan orders (useful for adherence gap analysis) |
| **`:below` server support** | Queries A1, A2, A4 require the server to support hierarchical token expansion. Verify with your FHIR server's CapabilityStatement. Fall back to A5 pattern if unsupported |
| **MedicationStatement** | Add parallel `MedicationStatement?code=...` queries (same RxNorm codes) to catch patient-reported or externally-documented triptan use |
| **MedicationDispense** | Optionally add `MedicationDispense?code=...` queries to identify patients who filled triptan prescriptions |
| **Diagnosis ∩ Medication** | Patients in *both* stream A and stream B are your highest-confidence migraine + triptan cohort; stream B–only patients may represent undocumented diagnoses warranting chart review |
